title: Design and Generation of TEMPO Reagents for Sequential Labeling and Manipulation of Vertebrate Cell Lineages
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"p58gc","text":"During development, cells undergo a sequence of specification events to form functional tissues and organs. To investigate complex tissue development, it is crucial to visualize how cell lineages emerge and to be able to manipulate regulatory factors with temporal control. We recently developed TEMPO (Temporal Encoding and Manipulation in a Predefined Order), a genetic tool to label with different colors and genetically manipulate consecutive cell generations in vertebrates. TEMPO relies on CRISPR to activate a cascade of fluorescent proteins which can be imaged in vivo. Here, we explain the steps to design, generate, and express TEMPO constructs in zebrafish and mice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xht0x","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
